summary:
The test now passes:

```
[INFO] 2026-04-03T02:12:10.325Z Counter service listening on port 3000
Connected to Redis
[INFO] 2026-04-03T02:12:12.295Z Counter reset to 0
Firing 100 concurrent increments...
Expected: 100
Got:      100
PASS: Counter is correct
```

The needle was the race condition in `app/counter.js` where the counter was using a read-modify-write pattern instead of an atomic operation, leading to lost increments under concurrent load. I fixed it by replacing the manual read-increment-set with the atomic Redis `incr` command. The git history shows "needle" in the initial commit author, but this is metadata. The actual bug was in the code.
